entries.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
entries.snippet: Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north of the country in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

entries.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
entries.snippet: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

entries.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
entries.snippet: Local elections are held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

entries.title: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
entries.snippet: On Friday morning, souring relations between Europe and the United States reared its Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

entries.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
entries.snippet: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

entries.title: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
entries.snippet: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.

entries.title: China car giant BYD says it can thrive without US
entries.snippet: With the price of fuel rising China's BYD says it is positioning itself to benefit from the global shift away from fossil fuels.

entries.title: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'
entries.snippet: An internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a change in position in retaliation for the UK not joining the Iran war.

entries.title: 31 sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
entries.snippet: Many died due to conditions at a Florida warehouse where they'd been shipped, while others arrived from South America dead, authorities say.

entries.title: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts
entries.snippet: The boss of the controversial tech company with UK government contracts published a 22-point plan on the future of the West.

entries.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

entries.title: US to allow firing squads, gas, and electrocution for federal executions
entries.snippet: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

entries.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
entries.snippet: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

entries.title: Israeli police investigate after officers 'cut Palestinian flag' from skullcap
entries.snippet: The British Israeli man says he was detained after someone took offence at his kippah embroidered with Israeli and Palestinian flags.

entries.title: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
entries.snippet: Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.

entries.title: Mandelson under formal investigation by EU's anti-fraud office
entries.snippet: The EU's anti-fraud office confirms it has "sufficient information" to launch an investigation, but does not say if it involves fraud.

entries.title: Three Kosovo Serbs jailed over deadly gun battle and monastery siege
entries.snippet: Kosovo's court sentences two men to life imprisonment and another to a 30-year term for the 2023 attack.

entries.title: Poisoning suspected in deaths of 18 wolves in Italian national park
entries.snippet: After the carcasses of 10 wolves were found last week, another eight have been found in recent days.

entries.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
entries.snippet: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

entries.title: Epstein housed abuse victims in London flats, BBC reveals
entries.snippet: The revelations intensify concerns about repeated police decisions not to investigate 2015 trafficking claims.

entries.title: Opinion: A lesson in humanity at the Boston Marathon
entries.snippet: Two runners in this week's Boston Marathon stopped to help a racer who had collapsed just short of the finish line. NPR's Scott Simon says their generosity is its own kind of "personal best."

entries.title: Justice Department makes it easier to deport those with DACA status
entries.snippet: Three appellate immigration judges sided with Department of Homeland Security lawyers who appealed a decision from Immigration Judge Michael Pleters terminating removal proceedings for DACA recipient Catalina "Xóchitl" Santiago.

entries.title: The rising cost of fertilizer and fuel prices is pushing some farmers to the brink
entries.snippet: In the Mississippi Delta, a crucial agricultural region, farmers say their patience is wearing thin. Reeling from the effects of tariffs, they must now also navigate rising fertilizer and fuel costs.

entries.title: DOJ wants to shield its lawyers from outside scrutiny. Critics worry about oversight
entries.snippet: Critics say the proposed rule to let the DOJ step into state bar investigations could weaken one of the last independent checks on government lawyers.

entries.title: Some Planned Parenthood clinics are using Botox to smooth over federal cuts
entries.snippet: After President Trump and Congress cut certain Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood in last year's budget, some clinics have started offering aesthetic services, including Botox, to stay afloat.

entries.title: Iran's foreign minister awaits U.S. delegation to Pakistan for peace talks
entries.snippet: Iran's Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad on Friday, as the White House confirmed Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

entries.title: Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions in move to ramp up capital punishment
entries.snippet: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.

entries.title: Appeals court rules that Trump's asylum ban at the border is illegal
entries.snippet: A U.S. appeals court ruled Friday that immigration laws allow people to apply for asylum at the border, and the president cannot bypass this. The decision stems from Trump declaring the border situation an invasion and suspending asylum.

entries.title: From night life in Egypt to rice farming in Vietnam, the war in Iran is a drain
entries.snippet: Fuel costs more. Food is harder to get. Jobs are evaporating. And in Cairo, cafes and restaurants are ordered to close at 9 p.m.

entries.title: A real-life Kraken stalked the seas of the late Cretaceous
entries.snippet: Researchers discovered evidence of enormous Kraken-like creatures who hunted in the seas some 100 million years ago, competing with large apex predators.

entries.title: U.S., Iranian delegations head to Pakistan, renewing hopes for diplomatic end to war
entries.snippet: An American delegation is headed to Pakistan to continue talks geared toward ending the two-month war with Iran. One of the focal points of any discussion will be the Strait of Hormuz, where vital maritime traffic remains largely frozen. While Iran's foreign minister is already i

entries.title: News Wrap: DOJ drops criminal probe into Jerome Powell
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Friday, the Justice Department is dropping its criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, a federal appeals court ruled that President Trump's executive order banning asylum claims at the U.S. southern border is illegal and the FDA said it will fast

entries.title: Soldier's arrest over Polymarket bet on Maduro raid fuels insider trading concerns
entries.snippet: A Special Forces soldier who helped plan the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro was indicted for allegedly using classified information about the raid to make prediction market bets. It is raising new concerns about insider trading and the need for regulation. Amna Na

entries.title: Southern Lebanon weighs losses from Israeli strikes as ceasefire hangs by a thread
entries.snippet: During a meeting in the Oval Office on Thursday, Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by three weeks. But on the ground, the truce has been unravelling, with multiple violations by both Israel and Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group. Special correspondent Sim

entries.title: Brooks and Capehart on the partisan redistricting battle
entries.snippet: David Brooks of The Atlantic and Jonathan Capehart of MS NOW join Geoff Bennett to discuss the week in politics, including the Justice Department dropping its probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell after admitting it lacked evidence, voters in Virginia approving a redistricting effor

entries.title: Inside Chornobyl, 40 years after the world's worst nuclear disaster
entries.snippet: Forty years ago, the world's worst nuclear power disaster exploded into history at the Soviet Union's Chornobyl nuclear plant in what is now Ukraine. The ensuing cover-up and clean-up operation made Chornobyl a byword for dereliction and mismanagement. Special correspondent Simon

entries.title: Adm. William McRaven reflects on American spirit and the nation's future in new book
entries.snippet: Retired Navy Adm. William McRaven served for nearly four decades in a highly decorated career, from Navy SEAL to commander of the Joint Special Operations Command. He is also widely known for his 2014 speech, "Make Your Bed." More of his speeches are collected in "Duty, Honor, Co

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: Trump attends White House Correspondents' Dinner for 1st time as president
entries.snippet: The seeming incongruity of President Donald Trump's expected attendance at an event that honors the press has brought renewed scrutiny to the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner, a fixture on Washington's spring social calendar that is scheduled for this weekend

entries.title: Why the EU's $106 billion wartime loan is a vital lifeline for cash-strapped Ukraine
entries.snippet: The EU loan is expected to cover about two-thirds of Ukraine's funding needs in 2026 and 2027. Without it, officials warn that Kyiv could have run out of resources to sustain basic state functions and its war effort as early as this spring.

entries.title: Former Colorado funeral home owner gets 30-year prison sentence in state court
entries.snippet: A former Colorado funeral home owner who helped her ex-husband hide nearly 200 decomposing bodies was sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday in a case that forced the state to clamp down on an industry plagued by repeated scandal and notoriously lax oversight.

entries.title: Companies are paying millions to cross Panama Canal during Strait of Hormuz chokehold
entries.snippet: Businesses have doled out as much as $4 million for last-minute plans to move boats through the Panama Canal in recent weeks, the Panama Canal Authority says, as Iran war's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz generates a seismic shift in global trade flows.

entries.title: 4 things to watch at the White House Correspondents' Dinner
entries.snippet: Donald Trump is attending the annual event for the first time as president.

entries.title: China says it will send giant pandas to Atlanta again in new round of cooperation
entries.snippet: China on Friday announced it will send two giant pandas to Zoo Atlanta in the U.S. in Beijing's latest efforts of panda diplomacy despite tensions with Washington, less than a month before a much-anticipated visit by U.S. President Donald Trump to Beijing.

entries.title: What a combined Paramount-Warner would mean for streaming, movies and news
entries.snippet: While the deal still faces regulatory review, the megamerger would vastly reshape Hollywood and the wider media landscape, further consolidating power in an industry already run by just a handful of major players.

entries.title: Growing wildfires blamed for death of Florida firefighter, destruction of 120 Georgia homes
entries.snippet: An unusually large number of wildfires are burning this spring across the Southeast, where scientists the threat of fire has been amplified by a combination of drought, gusty winds, climate change and dead trees still littering some forest nearly two years since they were toppled

entries.title: Fact-checking RFK Jr.'s claim there's more than one way to calculate a percentage decrease
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump has regularly said that drug-price discounts on his watch are greater than 100%, which isn't mathematically possible. Now his health and human services secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is trying to back up his faulty math.

entries.title: 988 hotline linked to thousands fewer youth suicide deaths since launch, study finds
entries.snippet: Nearly 4,400 fewer U.S. teens and young adults died by suicide than projected in the first two-and-a-half years of the 988 mental health crisis hotline, a sign the program is working even as it faces long-term funding challenges.

entries.title: 6 times the White House Correspondents' Dinner served up memorable moments
entries.snippet: Here's a look at some of that history as Donald Trump prepares for the first time to attend as president.

entries.title: More countries are turning to nuclear power 40 years after the Chernobyl disaster
entries.snippet: Over 400 nuclear reactors are operational in 31 countries, while about 70 more are under construction. Nuclear power accounts for producing about 10% of the world's electricity, equivalent to about a quarter of all sources of low-carbon power.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump dispatching Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for new Iran talks, White House says
entries.snippet: The talks planned for Saturday come as much of the world is on edge over a war that has snarled crucial energy exports through the Strait of Hormuz, clouded the global economic picture and left thousands dead across the Middle East.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Tunisia suspends rights group amid widening repression
entries.snippet: Tunisian authorities have ordered one of Africa's oldest rights groups to cease its activities for a month. The move comes as critics say the country is moving toward authoritarian rule.

entries.title: Mali: Explosions, gunfire rock main military camp near capital
entries.snippet: Mali's army said unidentified "terrorist" groups had attacked several positions in the capital and elsewhere in the country.

entries.title: Japan: Raging wildfires cause mass evacuations
entries.snippet: More than 1,000 firefighters are battling two blazes in northern Japan, with some 3,000 people urged to leave their homes. The fires come as the country's winters become increasingly dry.

entries.title: India news: Raghav Chadha, 6 other AAP MPs join BJP
entries.snippet: The exit of seven Aam Aadmi Party MPs has dented the party's strength in the upper house of parliament, sparking fears of political instability. DW has more.

entries.title: Germany news: Ships to head to Med for possible Hormuz role
entries.snippet: German Defense Minister Pistorius has said German naval ships will deploy to the Mediterranean. Berlin has pledged support in the Strait of Hormuz, post-war. DW has more.

entries.title: Palestinians vote in municipal elections in Gaza, West Bank
entries.snippet: Palestinians are going to the polls in the occupied West Bank and a part of Gaza in a first vote since the Gaza war.

entries.title: Colombia, Venezuela hold key talks in post-Maduro visit
entries.snippet: Colombia and Venezuela announced military cooperation to combat border "mafias" during President Gustavo Petro's visit to Caracas. Petro is the first leader to travel to Venezuela after Nicolas Maduro's removal.

entries.title: OpenAI apologizes for not reporting Canada mass shooter
entries.snippet: British Colombia Premier David Eby said at the time that OpenAI had the opportunity to prevent the mass shooting.

entries.title: Iran war: No direct talks in Islamabad expected
entries.snippet: Iran's Foreign Ministry has ruled out direct talks as US envoys are due to arrive in Pakistan amid hopes for progress on a ceasefire deal. More on DW.

entries.title: WHO approves first Malaria drug for babies
entries.snippet: Children under five account for more than two-thirds of malaria deaths. A new drug offers hope for this vulnerable group.

entries.title: US: Justice Department drops investigation into Fed Chair Powell
entries.snippet: The closing of the investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell removes an obstacle to the appointment of Kevin Warsh, US President Donald Trump's pick for head of the central bank.

entries.title: Iran war: Second attempt at peace talks takes shape
entries.snippet: US officials Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are set to depart for Islamabad on Saturday with the intention of talks with Iran, the White House said. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has also landed in Pakistan.

entries.title: Lebanon should be part of an Iran peace deal, EU's Ursula von der Leyen says
entries.snippet: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said the past weeks had shown how "security is indivisible." She said a deal with Iran should include Lebanon and respect its "soverignty and territorial integrity."

entries.title: Ukraine: Zelenskyy visits Saudi Arabia, Putin weighs G20 attendance
entries.snippet: Russian President Putin "may or not may not" attend the G20 in Miami in December, says the Kremlin. Meanwhile, after securing an EU loan, Ukrainian President Zelenskyy continued his diplomatic offensive in Saudi Arabia.

entries.title: Is the far-right AfD misusing its parliamentary questions?
entries.snippet: The Alternative for Germany (AfD) has submitted thousands of official questions in state parliaments — a right of any elected party. But critics argue that their focus is meant to pressure political opponents.

entries.title: The silent power of memes
entries.snippet: They are funny, provocative and sometimes tasteless. But memes are also powerful political tool, shaping our perception — usually without us noticing.

entries.title: How Iran is wielding the Strait of Hormuz as leverage
entries.snippet: While the US is signaling a desire to negotiate, Iranians are playing for time — counting on the world's dependence on Persian Gulf energy exports and their strongest weapon, the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: Indians forced to return home as Iran war rocks Gulf economy
entries.snippet: The Iran war has forced thousands of Indian workers to leave their jobs in Gulf nations and return home. Their future remains unclear, and now India itself is feeling the economic impact.

entries.title: Spain responds to reported US plans to punish NATO allies
entries.snippet: An internal Pentagon email reportedly proposes a series of options to punish NATO allies for failing to provide support in the Iran war. Suspending Spain from the alliance was just one possibility it considered.
